nativeRange.habitat: Subtidal and intertidal rocky reef; attaches via holdfast to hard substrate in warm tropical seas.

decorPreferences: hard substrate for holdfast attachment — wedge or attach with zip-tie/fishing line; do not bury in sand

avoidWith: herbivorous fish that may graze macroalgae (e.g. tangs, rabbitfish)

imageCandidates.notes: Philippe Bourjon; 1200x1007. In-water reef shot of a vibrant lime-to-dark green Codium sp. with the classic spongy, cylindrical, dichotomously-branching finger-like fronds — matches this genus/'sp.' entry's morphology exactly. Sharp, whole specimen, tropical setting.
Diego Delso; 2884x1923. In-situ underwater Codium tomentosum, single upright specimen showing forked finger-branches. Sharp, well-lit; represents a distinct species in the genus.
Esteban Tazzioli; 2048x1536. In-water Codium decorticatum, thick dark-green cylindrical branches over colorful reef substrate. Sharp; adds a distinct species.
Christian Ferrer; 3270x2616. Clean, sharp olive-green velvety sphere of Codium bursa — the distinctive ball-form species of the genus. Out of water but well-framed and vivid.
Jerry Kirkhart; 3020x2008. Codium fragile ('Dead Man's Fingers') in a tide pool, sharp, showing the sprawling spongy finger-fronds. Colour is a natural olive; adds a distinct species.
Neptali Ramirez Marcial; large size. Codium isthmocladum, green cylindrical fingered fronds. Washed up on sand but sharp and clearly the genus; adds a distinct tropical Atlantic species.
Thomas Irvine; 1638x2048. Codium edule, in-situ live green lobed/fingered thallus. Some sediment dusting and a tighter macro crop, so kept as a non-recommended extra; adds another distinct species.

summary: Stout Dwarf Codium is a compact, spongy green macroalgae with short, thick finger-like branches, considerably smaller than the typical aquarium-trade Codium at around 2–8 cm. It tends to thrive in standard reef parameters and is generally well suited to display tanks.
careNotes:
Stout Dwarf Codium is a dwarf-form Codium sp. available in two color variants: a darker, more compact 'Stout Dwarf' form and a paler 'Lime Green' form. Both are considerably smaller than the typical aquarium-trade Codium, reportedly reaching roughly 2–8 cm. Flow appears to influence branching structure — higher flow tends to produce a shorter, more compact arrangement, while lower flow encourages tighter branching — suggesting moderate to lower flow is generally advisable for specimen health.

Despite belonging to the order Bryopsidales alongside calcified genera such as Halimeda and Penicillus, Codium tissue is spongy and NOT calcified — no calcium supplementation is required for growth directly.

Lighting: full-spectrum LED is recommended; actinic-only spectrum has been noted to be insufficient for more demanding macroalgae. Medium intensity has been reported as suitable for Codium.

Attachment: should be attached to hard substrate via wedging, zip-tie/fishing line, or superglue. Do NOT bury the base in sand — unlike Caulerpa and calcareous greens, Codium is not a sand-bed genus and may melt at the base if buried. Weekly dosing of iron and iodine trace elements is generally advised for all macroalgae.

Cross-reference with sibling species: Codium fragile ('Dead Man's Fingers', [sw-algae-032]) is a cool-water species preferring 10–24°C and is distinctly different from this warm-water Codium sp. Do not use C. fragile as a care reference for this species.
breedingNotes: In aquarium settings, fragmentation is the practical propagation method. Frond fragments can generally establish on hard substrate when placed in moderate flow with adequate lighting.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros — Dwarf Stout Codium
primary.notes: Primary trade source. Confirms compact dark-form Codium sp. identity, short finger-like branch morphology, flow response, and 1–2" cutting/golf-ball clump sizing. Mosaic is the sole aquarium trade source identified for this entry.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Macroalgae Care Guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source. Confirms warm reef parameter targets (temp 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, Ca 400–450 ppm, Mg 1250–1350 ppm, NO3 5–20 ppm, PO4 0.02–0.1 ppm), full-spectrum lighting requirement, propagation methods, and sand-bed melt warning for non-Caulerpa/non-calcareous green genera. Meets 2-source minimum for this Mosaic-only entry.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Lime Green Codium (secondary listing)
additional.notes: Second Mosaic SKU consolidated into this entry. Describes a paler, smaller Codium sp. form; confirms genus-level identification and that both forms are distinct from typical full-sized Codium.
